title: Teacher and Student Models
concept: Distillation trains a smaller student model to imitate a larger teacher model, so the student can keep some of the teacher’s behavior while using less memory and compute.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why might a team pick a slightly less accurate model for a phone app?

options.text: Because it may fit the phone’s latency, memory, and battery limits much better.
Because smaller models never need evaluation.
Because phones can only run linear models.

exercises.explain: Correct. Once you know smaller models matter, the next question is how to teach one to behave more like a strong larger model.

cards.title: A teacher shows more than right or wrong
cards.body: A good teacher does not just mark an answer correct or incorrect. They also show which wrong answers were close, which ideas are related, and where confidence should be high or low. Distillation brings that idea into deep learning.

cards.title: Teacher and student models
cards.body: In knowledge distillation, a large, strong teacher model produces guidance, and a smaller student model learns to imitate that behavior. Instead of learning only from hard labels, the student also learns from the teacher’s output pattern, which can transfer some of the teacher’s knowledge into a lighter model.

cards.title: A music coach, not just a score sheet
cards.body: A student violinist learns more from a coach who demonstrates timing, emphasis, and phrasing than from a sheet that only says “pass” or “fail.” A teacher model gives that richer demonstration.

cards.title: Teaching a phone assistant
cards.body: Suppose a large speech model running on servers understands accents very well, but it is too heavy for a phone. A team can train a smaller student to imitate the teacher’s predictions so the phone model keeps some of that quality while running much faster.

cards.title: The student is not copying weights line by line
cards.body: A common myth is that distillation means making an exact miniature clone of the teacher. Not true. The student usually has a different size or architecture. It learns to match the behavior of the teacher, not to duplicate every internal detail.

cards.title: Labels only vs. teacher guidance
cards.body: The student can learn more when it sees the teacher’s full output pattern, not just the final answer.

left.text:
Learns the final class.
Less information about close alternatives.
Standard supervised training signal.

right.text:
Learns the final class plus the teacher’s confidence pattern.
Sees which alternatives look similar.
Can absorb richer behavior.

exercises.prompt: What is the main idea of knowledge distillation?

options.text: Train a smaller student model to imitate a stronger teacher model.
Delete all labels so the student learns randomly.
Force the teacher to use fewer training examples than the student.
Make both models identical in size so no tradeoff exists.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Distillation is about transferring behavior from a larger teacher into a smaller student.

exercises.prompt: True or false: In distillation, the student model must have exactly the same architecture and size as the teacher.
exercises.explain: False. The student is usually smaller on purpose.

exercises.sentence: Distillation often trains a smaller ___ model from a larger teacher.
exercises.explain: Student is correct. The whole point is to pass knowledge into a lighter learner.

options.text: A team uses a strong server model to guide training of a smaller mobile model.
A team removes its labels and hopes a random student will guess the task.
A team makes the teacher larger and calls that compression.

exercises.explain: Right. Distillation is useful when a strong teacher can supervise a compact student for deployment.

exercises.prompt: A company has an excellent but slow image model in the cloud. It wants a faster phone version without throwing away all the teacher’s learned behavior. What is the best plan?

options.text: Use distillation so a smaller student learns to imitate the strong teacher.
Delete the teacher and train a random tiny model with no supervision at all.
Keep only the largest possible model, even if it cannot run on the phone.
Assume model size never affects deployment speed.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Distillation is designed for this situation: a strong teacher, a smaller target device, and a need to transfer useful behavior.
